untranslatable_reason: This signal describes a volatility skew adjustment strategy that requires: (1) real-time implied volatility opinion tracking and comparison to market levels (not available in allowed indicators), (2) volatility skew shape modeling and positioning (requires multi-strike option data and skew surface management, not available), (3) directional position adjustment based on underlying moves combined with IV opinion shifts (requires portfolio Greeks and skew dynamics not expressible with price/technical indicators only). The signal is fundamentally about options portfolio management and volatility surface positioning, which cannot be translated into entry/exit rules using only price-based technical indicators. Required inputs: implied volatility levels by strike, skew shape parameters, current portfolio position Greeks—none available in the allowed indicator set.
translation_notes: Signal ID NATENBERG_DRY_192_0 references volatility skew adjustment framework from Sheldon Natenberg's work on options trading. The core concept requires tracking IV opinion relative to market consensus (threshold ±0.25 vols) and adjusting skew position/shape dynamically. This is an advanced options portfolio management strategy incompatible with price-action technical analysis. A backtest engine supporting this would need implied volatility term structure data, option Greeks, and skew surface modeling capabilities.
